column7: Notes

column1:
Cells in this row are description for the Gemini Agent.
Cell contains an explanation, for you, of what the column of the cell represents.
In each column, the first row is the name, the second row is the description of the column for the Gemini Agent, and the rest is data. Row n (n > 2) is data for the study spot in the same row.
column3: Coordinates, as shown by google maps
column5: Show as a subjective rating. Color code it so really good is #0a53a8, pretty good is #bfe1f6, OK is #bba897, not great is #000000, not visited is #ffae00.
column6: How busy the spot usually is. Color code it so usually isolated is #5a3286, there's some people here is #d4edbc, very popular is #b10202.
column7: Show this as text at the very bottom of a study spot's page.
column8: This is the description of the study spot for the user. It should come first on the page of the study spot, before any bullet points.
column9: Items to the right of this column are yes/y or no/n. The description contains the name of the associated icon, like plug.png, to be displayed next to a bullet point on the study spot's site. If yes, show the icon with a green tint. If no, show the icon with a red tint and a red line across it. If empty, do not show the bullet point at all.
column15: Items to the right of this column are bonuses. If these are yes/y, show them and the associated icon at the bottom of the bullet-point list with no tint. If they are no or empty, do not show the bullet point.

column7: Clean, fantastic vibes
Often closed
AKA Harry Potter Room.
Usually packed during peak hours
Good if you need empty classrooms
Nice vibes - IKB-like.
Very fancy.
Hallway tables always empty. Loud machinery.
Dead silent, always has seats.
Lonely cubical
Kettles and microwaves available
A few seats on L1 and a couch on L2. Two classes on L1 which are hit or miss (sometimes locked)

Permanently closed.
Rotten fr
Extremely tall roof. Cafe + food nearby.
Good if you need empty classrooms
Many private but frequently booked rooms. Labs are always open and good for locking in (I’ve never been kicked out). Allegedly, cheap chips at downstairs student lounge.
Second floor ⭐⭐⭐
Lower floor really pretty
Cool if you wanna be alone but in a community place
Available in basement, main floor behind lounge, and near L4 lecture hall. All bookable, so you may get kicked out.
Couches & tables, but perpetually dirty. More often free than one may guess.
Very dirty and loud. Usually some tables open. Upstairs is nicer.
Great soundproofing, heater present, great acoustics, TV. One on main + upper floors.
No tables, but among the coziest public spots on campus.
Wide, open room with glass walls. Not many outlets.
Free drinks available
Always available, but near faculty offices. Free drink coffee, hot chocolate, etc on 3rd floor.
Many private spaces. At least one room always open.
Large lecture hall usually not used
Cold tables, very welcoming vibe
Couches and tables
You will sometimes find empty rooms
Usually a few empty rooms.
Rooms are bookable WITHOUT 24h notice.
Some private rooms, but always full
2 private rooms, but public area always has space. Decently quite.
Usually some people in there, but very quite. Technically not public.
You are bound to find empty classrooms, but they are very old, cold, and rocky.
Lots of classrooms, less used than usual. Good views, old vibes. Public areas too!
Lots of classrooms, plenty of space. Student-homey vibes.
No rooms, only a few one-person couches and tables.
A few public tables that usually have space. Couches that are usually empty. Children frequent this space.
Tables on higher level usually have space. Public study space often has only a few empty seats, but lots of trees! Classrooms are sometimes empty.
Hard to find empty classroom. Public study spots are few and far between.
Downstairs has seats that are never used and dead silent. No spaces otherwise.
Classrooms, though frequently used, are nice after hours. Very modern. Some public spaces. 4th floor spaces less often used.
Only very busy (often 100% full) public space. The left door of the upstairs hall is broken (sometimes doesn’t lock).
Level 1 has very busy public spots. Level 2 has slightly quieter public spots and some class/lab rooms.
Has a single private room on level 2. Public areas are nice, but pretty busy.
Very fancy public area with very fancy personal lights and plugs. Quite even though it’s busy.
Public spot. Always has empty tables.

column4: School of Population and Public Health (SPPH)
column7: Downstairs classrooms are vibes, super private, and NOT BOOKABLE YESSSSS. Always full though…
Some private rooms on main floor, but usually full. The smaller lecture halls are nice. Public area is noisy but comfortable.
Main floor always has space. Cozy spot behind the librarian counter. Downstairs is super vibes with the empty computer lab and silent space. Upstairs has tables and bookable rooms too.
Among the nicest lounges at UBC. Comfy, but usually busy.
A few classrooms. A secret meeting room is available upstairs.
Has 1st, 2nd, and 3rd floor public areas which are nice but busy. Secret classroom on floor 3. Nice lecture hall.
3rd floor. Quietest student lounge ever. Has tables and a good view of the IRC backyard.
Has several floor of public spots. Non-private private rooms.
Downstairs has couches and tall tables only, but it’s nice. Nice fireplaces for chilling. Nothing upstairs.
